single_concern_assessment: v4 honestly drops the single-concern claim and reframes as 'multi-concern by-design'. The circularity argument for AC-1+AC-4+AC-7 is genuinely defensible (pre-commit hook calls verify-slice.sh; AC-7 ships in same harness layer; dogfooding-against-self). However, the bundle-keeping rationale for AC-3 + AC-6 + AC-8 is weaker — the reviewer's option (a) of extracting them to v3a-ii sibling is dismissed on 'compliance theatre' grounds, but AC-3 (ESLint) + AC-6 (coverage gates) + AC-8 (CLAUDE.md stub) have NO dependency cycle with the foundation triad. The 'cost > benefit' tradeoff is asserted, not demonstrated. Label-of-convenience risk is mitigated (not eliminated) by §10's explicit audit trail.

v3_finding_response_audit.note: Single-concern claim dropped honestly; bundle still keeps AC-3/6/8 on weak rationale.
Random-nonce per invocation via /dev/urandom + {NONCE} substitution; meta-test asserts randomness.
Notes §186 documents rename-vs-controls sequence; HANDOFF-SESSION-37 carries note.
Session column added; 780+630+150=1560 reconciles row-by-row exactly.
All 7 pre-AC-1 commits enumerated; rule reframed to 'first src/.claude/hooks/scripts commit onwards'.
Cross-session plan expanded with explicit S-37-1..S-37-7 + S-38-1..S-38-5 ordered sub-sequence.
Notes §187 confirms git show --stat 7affd8f touched both v3a + v3c stub.
AC-6 row raised 80→120 covering shellspec install + ts-script + CI step.

findings.description: Bundle-keeping rationale for AC-3 + AC-6 + AC-8 is asserted, not demonstrated. The §10 paragraph correctly identifies AC-1+AC-4+AC-7 as circularly dependent, then conflates that with AC-3/AC-6/AC-8 to justify keeping the bundle whole. AC-3 (ESLint config + allowlist + CI denial) is a static-analysis configuration with no dependency on verify-slice.sh — it could ship in a predecessor or sibling slice. AC-6 (vitest coverage thresholds) is similarly independent: the lcov-parser INSIDE verify-slice.sh depends on coverage config existing, but coverage config does not depend on verify-slice.sh. AC-8 (CLAUDE.md stub) is documentation. The 'compliance theatre' framing is rhetorical — extracting these would genuinely yield a smaller, more reviewable foundation triad of ~860 lines (50+100+120+60+170+80+100 docs+AC-1+AC-2+AC-4+AC-7+AC-8 wait — even AC-8 is doc).
findings.recommendation: Either (a) extract AC-3 + AC-6 + AC-8 to a v3a-ii sibling slice landing first (gives v3a-foundation a smaller dogfood scope and clean predecessor); or (b) strengthen §10 with a concrete demonstration of the dependency: e.g. 'AC-1's verify-slice.sh requires coverage thresholds in vitest.config.ts to parse lcov; without AC-6 landing first or in the same slice, AC-1's coverage gate has nothing to enforce'. Option (b) is partial — only AC-6 has that argument; AC-3 and AC-8 still don't. Currently the rationale handwaves all three together.

findings.description: Nonce-substitution mechanism in AC-7 is under-specified for security-relevant code. §41 says 'subagent prompt template uses literal placeholder {NONCE} substituted at runtime' but does not specify: (i) where substitution happens (in shell before subagent invocation? inside subagent process?), (ii) whether the substituted prompt is logged anywhere the author could read post-invocation (if Claude writes intermediate prompts to disk per harness convention, the author could grep the log on a subsequent invocation to learn the prior nonce — though prior-nonce knowledge doesn't bypass current invocation), (iii) entropy of `head -c 16 /dev/urandom | xxd -p` (32 hex chars / 128 bits — adequate, but should be stated), (iv) what happens if /dev/urandom is unavailable in the test sandbox (fallback? hard-fail? meta-test must cover this). The belt-and-braces 'subagent treats unknown nonce as plan content' is the right idea but its prompt-engineering robustness is not specified.
findings.recommendation: AC-7 mechanism §41 should state: (a) NONCE generated by exit-plan-review.sh BEFORE plan content is read from stdin/argv (so author cannot influence nonce derivation); (b) substitution via shell parameter expansion in heredoc, NOT via sed/awk on plan content (which would be re-substitutable); (c) entropy 128 bits explicit; (d) /dev/urandom unavailability is hard-fail with explicit error; (e) meta-test covers fake-nonce-injection AND missing-/dev/urandom AND nonce-collision-on-rapid-reinvocation. security.md threat model section enumerates these per spec 72 §11 box 11 (adversarial review).

findings.description: §Pre-AC-1 exempt-commits rule states 'Plus the v4-revision commit (this commit) and any v4+ review-capture commits before AC-1 lands' but the listed table at §15 ends at 7cf96c7 and does not name 8a79752. A future reader (or v5 reviewer) cross-checking `git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD` against the table will find an extra commit not in the list. Documentation drift risk.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) extend the table at §15 to include 8a79752 with annotation '— v4 revision (this commit)'; or (b) add a footnote 'table reflects state at v3-review-capture commit 7cf96c7; v4-revision commit 8a79752 itself is also exempt per the open-ended rule below.' Trivial fix; flagged because exemption-list precision is the entire point of H5 closure.

findings.description: S-37-2 skeleton claim 'passes against itself AND against docs/slices/S-F7-alpha-contracts-dev-mode/ reference slice (DoD-2 closure)' is verifiable today: α has all four artefacts (acceptance.md + security.md + verification.md + test-plan.md confirmed via ls). Skeleton's file-presence-only check would pass against α trivially. Sub-sequence is structurally sound. Minor concern: skeleton mode is 'permissive — does NOT enforce coverage / function-size / TDD yet' which means commits S-37-3 through S-37-7 effectively pass through a no-op gate. The DoD-2 invariant in session 37 is therefore weak (file-presence only); real DoD-2 verification waits for S-38-1's full-impl. State this trade-off explicitly so a reader doesn't assume session-37-end represents real gate enforcement.
findings.recommendation: Add to End-of-session-37 line: 'Note: skeleton mode is file-presence-only; full gate enforcement (coverage / lint / TDD) lands in S-38-1. Session-37 commits dogfooded against the wiring, not the rules.'

summary: v4 substantially addresses v3 — H2 BLOCK closed via random-nonce framing, H4 budget arithmetic now reconciles row-by-row exactly (780+630+150=1560), H6 ordered sub-sequence specified, H5/H3/H7/H8 closed cleanly. However, two request-changes remain: I1 (multi-concern bundle-keeping rationale handwaves AC-3/AC-6/AC-8 together with the genuinely-circular AC-1/AC-4/AC-7 — these three could and arguably should extract to v3a-ii) and I2 (nonce-substitution mechanism is conceptually right but missing security-relevant specification: substitution timing, entropy, /dev/urandom-fallback, log-leakage). Plus 2 nits (I3 exemption table omits this commit; I4 skeleton-mode permissive trade-off should be stated). Recommended: iterate-v5 with tightened §10 rationale (or split-and-ship) + AC-7 nonce-mechanism spec hardening. After v5 nit-or-approve verdict, proceed to AC-1 impl per H6 sub-sequence.
